message:
Embed RPC server as a Vite dev plugin — npm run dev is enough

Drop the requirement for a second tab running `npm run server`. The
new web/vite-rpc-server.mjs plugin attaches a noServer-mode
WebSocketServer to Vite's underlying http.Server's `upgrade` event
and routes /capnwasm + /capnweb to the bench backends. Vite's HMR WS
keeps working unchanged because we only handle the two bench paths.

Web/src/rpc/main.ts now connects to ws://HOST:PORT (same origin) so
the page works from `npm run dev` with no other setup. Static
`vite preview` still requires `npm run server` since preview is just
file-serving.

Verified end-to-end via headless Chromium (vite alone, no separate
server process):

  burst:    11 µs/call vs 60 µs/call   capnwasm 5.45× faster
  pipe:     700 µs vs 700 µs           tied (localhost noise)
  blob:     1.5 ms vs 1.2 ms (~128 KB vs ~171 KB wire)

The burst win is consistent across runs; pipeline + blob fluctuate
within localhost RTT noise but capnwasm always sends fewer wire bytes.
